metadata.scheme_name: Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme for Class 11th

raw_data.details: The "Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme Class 11th" by the Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to encourage girls from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities to pursue education. Under this scheme, an incentive amount of ₹3,000 is provided to girls enrolling in Class 11 for the first time. The scholarship distribution is managed through the Directorate of Public Distribution, Government of Madhya Pradesh.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityGirls Literacy Promotion Scheme for Class 11thMadhya PradeshGirls Literacy Promotion Scheme for Class 11thEducationFemaleFinancialGirlPre MatricSchoolStudentCheck EligibilityDetailsThe "Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme Class 11th" by the Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to encourage girls from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities to pursue education. Under this scheme, an incentive amount of ₹3,000 is provided to girls enrolling in Class 11 for the first time. The scholarship distribution is managed through the Directorate of Public Distribution, Government of Madhya Pradesh. BenefitsAn incentive of ₹3,000. EligibilityThe applicant should be from Denotified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic Community. The applicant should be a girl. The applicant should be enrolled in the 11th Standard. The applicant should be studying in a recognized institution. The applicant should have studied continuously without any academic breaks. Application ProcessOnlineRegistrationStep 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)". Step 2: Navigate to the top right corner of the homepage and click "[Registration".](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default)Step 3: On the registration page, provide the mandatory details. (the fields marked with * are mandatory). If required, verify your Mobile Number and/or Email ID via OTP. Finally, click "Register". ApplicationStep 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)". Step 2: Use the credentials you created during registration to log in. Step 3: Navigate to the online application form of the scheme you intend to apply for. Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Step 5: Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections. Step 6: Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any). Step 7: Click the "Submit" to submit your application. Documents RequiredPassport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste CertificateSamagra IDBank Account DetailsPrevious Year MarksheetsProof of Enrollment into 11th StandardBonafide Certificate of a Recognised InstitutionFrequently Asked QuestionsWho is eligible for the Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme? The applicant should be a girl from De-notified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes and enrolled in Class 11 for the first time. What is the incentive amount provided under the scheme? An incentive amount of ₹3,000 is provided to eligible girls. Is the scheme available for students in private institutions? The scheme is applicable only for students studying in recognized institutions as per the guidelines. Do students need to reapply for the incentive every year? No, the incentive is provided as a one-time benefit when enrolling in Class 11. What happens if a student discontinues their studies? The applicant must be continuously studying; discontinuing studies may disqualify them from receiving the incentive. How is the incentive distributed? The incentive is distributed through the Directorate of Public Instruction under the Integrated Scholarship Scheme. Is this scheme applicable to boys? No, the scheme is specifically designed to promote girls' education. Can a student receive this incentive if they repeat Class 11? No, the incentive is only for students enrolling in Class 11 for the first time. Are there any specific income criteria for eligibility? The scheme does not mention any income criteria, but the student must belong to De-notified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes. Can students apply for other scholarships along with this scheme? Yes, students may be eligible for other scholarships, depending on their qualifications and criteria. Sources And References[Scheme Details](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/SchemeDetail? ID=1)[Registration](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default.aspx)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be from Denotified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic Community. The applicant should be a girl. The applicant should be enrolled in the 11th Standard. The applicant should be studying in a recognized institution. The applicant should have studied continuously without any academic breaks.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Navigate to the top right corner of the homepage and click "[Registration".](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default)
Step 3: On the registration page, provide the mandatory details. (the fields marked with * are mandatory). If required, verify your Mobile Number and/or Email ID via OTP. Finally, click "Register". Application
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Use the credentials you created during registration to log in.
Step 3: Navigate to the online application form of the scheme you intend to apply for.
Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
Step 5: Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections.
Step 6: Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any).
Step 7: Click the "Submit" to submit your application.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste CertificateSamagra IDBank Account DetailsPrevious Year MarksheetsProof of Enrollment into 11th StandardBonafide Certificate of a Recognised Institution

raw_data.scheme_name: Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme for Class 11th

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant should be a girl from De-notified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes and enrolled in Class 11 for the first time.

faqs.question: What is the incentive amount provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: An incentive amount of ₹3,000 is provided to eligible girls.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available for students in private institutions?
faqs.answer: The scheme is applicable only for students studying in recognized institutions as per the guidelines.

faqs.question: Do students need to reapply for the incentive every year?
faqs.answer: No, the incentive is provided as a one-time benefit when enrolling in Class 11.

faqs.question: What happens if a student discontinues their studies?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be continuously studying; discontinuing studies may disqualify them from receiving the incentive.

faqs.question: How is the incentive distributed?
faqs.answer: The incentive is distributed through the Directorate of Public Instruction under the Integrated Scholarship Scheme.

faqs.question: Is this scheme applicable to boys?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is specifically designed to promote girls' education.

faqs.question: Can a student receive this incentive if they repeat Class 11?
faqs.answer: No, the incentive is only for students enrolling in Class 11 for the first time.

faqs.question: Are there any specific income criteria for eligibility?
faqs.answer: The scheme does not mention any income criteria, but the student must belong to De-notified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes.

faqs.question: Can students apply for other scholarships along with this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, students may be eligible for other scholarships, depending on their qualifications and criteria.
